comment_text.sn22.47:2.1: Read avigataṁ (“not rid”) here and SN 22.89:5.9; cf. AN 6.13:6.2, DN 33:2.2.106, DN 34:1.7.63.
comment_text.sn22.47:2.2: From here the discourse alludes to dependent origination. The sense faculties are “conceived”, i.e. “take rebirth”, like name and form (SN 12.39:1.4) or consciousness (SN 12.59:1.2) or an embryo (MN 93:18.61).
comment_text.sn22.47:2.4: The “mind” (mano) in the presence of “ignorance” makes choices that drive the cycle (SN 12.25:10.3). Here mano, as usual, signifies the active volitional dimension of mind, while ignorance is shown to be not self-existent, but part of the complex of mental activity.
comment_text.sn22.47:2.5: Through “contact” and “feeling” there arises the “grasping” of views of self.
comment_text.sn22.47:2.6: See Uddālaka’s, “I am that, I indeed am that” (iyam aham asmīyam aham asmīti, Chāndogya Upaniṣad 6.10.1) and “I am” (ahamasmi) at Sāman Veda 594.
comment_text.sn22.47:3.1: This is the one who ends the cycle.

root_text.sn22.47:1.2: “Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā anekavihitaṁ attānaṁ samanupassamānā samanupassanti, sabbete pañcupādānakkhandhe samanupassanti, etesaṁ vā aññataraṁ.
root_text.sn22.47:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano ariyānaṁ adassāvī ariyadhammassa akovido ariyadhamme avinīto, sappurisānaṁ adassāvī sappurisadhammassa akovido sappurisadhamme avinīto
root_text.sn22.47:1.5: rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.47:1.9: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ; attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn22.47:2.1: Iti ayañceva samanupassanā ‘asmī’ti cassa avigataṁ hoti.
root_text.sn22.47:2.2: ‘Asmī’ti kho pana, bhikkhave, avigate pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ avakkanti hoti—
root_text.sn22.47:2.4: Atthi, bhikkhave, mano, atthi dhammā, atthi avijjādhātu.
root_text.sn22.47:2.5: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa ‘asmī’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn22.47:3.2: Athettha sutavato ariyasāvakassa avijjā pahīyati, vijjā uppajjati.

translation_text.sn22.47:1.2: “Mendicants, whatever ascetics and brahmins regard various kinds of things as self, all regard the five grasping aggregates, or one of them.
translation_text.sn22.47:1.4: It’s when an unlearned ordinary person has not seen the noble ones, and is neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the noble ones. They’ve not seen true persons, and are neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the true persons.
translation_text.sn22.47:1.5: They regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn22.47:1.9: consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.47:2.1: So they’re not rid of this way of regarding things, and the conceit ‘I am’.
translation_text.sn22.47:2.2: As long as they’re not rid of the conceit ‘I am’, the five faculties are conceived—
translation_text.sn22.47:2.3: the eye, ear, nose, tongue, and body.
translation_text.sn22.47:2.4: The mind, ideas, and the element of ignorance are all present.
translation_text.sn22.47:2.5: Struck by feelings born of contact with ignorance, an unlearned ordinary person thinks ‘I am’,
translation_text.sn22.47:2.13: ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient’.
translation_text.sn22.47:3.1: The five faculties stay right where they are.
translation_text.sn22.47:3.2: But a learned noble disciple gives up ignorance about them and gives rise to knowledge.
translation_text.sn22.47:3.3: With the fading away of ignorance and the arising of knowledge, they don’t think ‘I am’,
translation_text.sn22.47:3.11: ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient’.”

variant_text.sn22.47:3.1: Tiṭṭhanteva kho → tiṭṭhanti kho pana (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | tattheva → tatheva (katthaci)
